title: Multimission and multispectral sonar
abstract: A survey system including a multibeam echo sounder having a single projector array and a single hydrophone array constructs a multi-signal message and deconstructs a corresponding multi-signal echo to substantially simultaneously perform multiple survey missions.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A survey system for performing multiple missions per message cycle, the survey system including a multibeam echo sounder system for installation on a water going vehicle, the survey system comprising: an acoustic transceiver for use with one or more transducers in a single projector array and plural transducers in a single hydrophone array! the projector array arranged with respect to the hydrophone array to form a Mills Cross! the transceiver for use with a plurality of N non-overlapping frequency bands having respective bandwidths and center frequencies; the transceiver for synthesizing a transmitter message that incorporates one or more signals from each of the frequency bands, the signals supporting a plurality of missions; and, the message for exciting the projector array such that a swath of a waterbody bottom is ensonified by each of the signals in the message and a message echo from ensonified scattering centers is returned to the hydrophone array; wherein a first of the frequency bands is for supporting a first mission and a second of the frequency bands is for supporting a second mission, the first mission frequency band being widely spaced apart from the second mission frequency band to promote survey system recognition of one or more frequency dependent characteristics of the ensonified scattering centers. 2. The survey system of claim 1 wherein the scattering centers include one or more of surface scattering centers at the waterbody bottom, volumetric scattering centers in the waterbody sub -bottom, and volumetric scattering centers in the waterbody water column. 3. The survey system of claim 1 wherein survey system recognition of backscatter strength at a particular projector angle relative to vertical is promoted. 4. The survey system of claim 1 wherein survey system recognition of average angular backscatter strength is promoted. 5. The survey system of claim 1 wherein any two of the center frequencies are separated by at least twice the bandwidth of the narrowest of the corresponding signal bandwidths or by at least 30% of the lower of the two center frequencies. 6. The survey system of claim 1 wherein the water column or waterbody bottom is ensonified substantially simultaneously by each of the signals in the message. 7. The survey system of claim 1 wherein the water column or waterbody bottom are ensonified in series, either in a single message cycle or across multiple message cycles. 8. The survey system of claim 1 further comprising: in the transmitter, N signal generators for generating the signals included in the message! and, in the receiver, for each hydrophone a parallel input filter bank including N digitally implemented bandpass filters with respective center frequencies corresponding to the band center frequencies. 9. The survey system of claim 1 further comprising: a first mission facility for bathymetry and a second mission facility for bathymetry; the first mission facility using one of the frequency bands centered 

 at a lower frequency; and, the second mission facility using one of the frequency bands centered at a higher frequency! wherein the center frequency of the lower frequency band is lower than the center frequency of the higher frequency band. 10. The survey system of claim 9 wherein: the lower frequency band has a center frequency in the range of a 180-450 kHz for ensonifying a swath having a first across track beamwidth and a first across track swath width; and, the higher frequency band has a center frequency in the range of 650-850 kHz for ensonifying a swath having a second across track beamwidth and a second across track swath width where the first swath width is greater than the second swath width and second beamwidth is less than the first beamwidth. 11. The survey system of claim 10 wherein the message includes one or both of a continuous wave signal and a frequency modulated signal. 12. The survey system of claim 9 wherein the message includes an orthogonal spread spectrum signal. 13. The survey system of claim 9 wherein the message includes a phase coded signal. 14. The survey system of claim 9 wherein the message includes a pulse train signal. 15. The survey system of claim 9 wherein the message includes a low probability of intercept signal. 16. The survey system of claim 9 wherein the message echo 

 includes first mission backscatter from first mission source of echoes and second mission backscatter from second mission source of echoes, the second mission source of echoes being a subset of the first mission source of echoes. 17. The survey system of claim 8 further comprising: first, second, and third mission facilities for waterbody bottom segmentation or characterization; the first mission facility using a first one of the frequency bands in a first frequency range! the second mission facility using a second one of the frequency bands in a second frequency range; and, the third mission facility using a third one of the frequency bands in a third frequency range! wherein the center frequency of the second frequency band is higher than that of the first frequency band and lower than that of the third frequency band. 18. The survey system of claim 17 wherein statistically significant differences between frequency dependent properties of backscatter are used to deconstruct backscatter data resulting from the transmitted message into backscatter datasets corresponding to the first, second, and third missions. 19. The survey system of claim 17 wherein each of the signals in the first, second, and third frequency bands is a continuous wave signal. 20. The survey system of claim 18 wherein the first frequency band is centered at about 100 kHz, the second frequency band is centered at about 200 kHz, and the third frequency band is centered at about 400 kHz. 21. The survey system of claim 20 wherein the waterbody bottom segmentation or characterization is based on echoes from a region of the bottom that is common to the first, second, and third frequency band signals. 22. The survey system of claim 20 further comprising; for each signal, a corresponding backscatter data set derived from backscatter data; for one of the signals in the first, second, or third frequency bands, the corresponding backscatter dataset used to segment the waterbody bottom at intervals along a swath ensonified by the signal; for another of the signals in the first, second, or third frequency bands, the corresponding backscatter data set used to refine the earlier segmentation; and, for another of the signals in the first, second or third frequency bands, the corresponding backscatter data set used to refine at least one of the earlier segmentations. 23. The survey system of claim 17 further comprising: a fourth mission facility for bathymetry; and, the fourth mission facility using a fourth one of the frequency bands in a fourth frequency range; wherein the center frequency of the fourth frequency band is higher than that of the third frequency band. 24. The survey system of claim 23 wherein each of the signals in the first, second, and third frequency bands is a continuous wave signal and the signal in the fourth frequency band is a frequency modulated signal. 25. The survey system of claim 24 wherein the first frequency band is centered at about 50 kHz, the second frequency band is centered 

 at about 150 kHz, the third frequency band is centered at about 250 kHz and the fourth frequency band is centered at about 400 kHz. 26. The survey system of claim 25 wherein the waterbody bottom segmentation or characterization is based on echoes from a region of the bottom that is common to the first, second, third and fourth frequency band signals. 27. The survey system of claim 8 further comprising: a first mission facility for navigation and a second mission facility for bathymetry; the first mission facility using a first one of the frequency bands in a first frequency range! and, the second mission facility using a second one of the frequency bands in a second frequency range; wherein the center frequency of the second frequency band is higher than that of the first frequency band. 28. The survey system of claim 27 wherein there are is at least one phase coded signal in the first frequency band and plural orthogonal spread spectrum signals in the second frequency band. 29. The survey system of claim 28 wherein the first frequency band is centered between 180 and 450 kHz and the second frequency band is centered between 650 and 850 kHz. 30. The survey system of claim 1 further comprising: a first mission facility for sub -bottom profiling and a second mission facility for bathymetry; the first mission facility using one of the frequency bands centered at a lower frequency; and, the second mission facility using one of the frequency bands centered at a higher frequency; 

 wherein the center frequency of the lower frequency band is lower than the center frequency of the higher frequency band. 31. The survey system of claim 30 wherein the lower center frequency is in a range of about 10 to 30 kHz and the higher center frequency is in a range of about 100 to 400 kHz.
